items.caption: A close-up view of a vehicle's windshield, with a focus on the reflection of the sky. the reflection is described as being horizontally polarized, which is a common occurrence when light from the sky is reflected by a windshield at an angle. this can cause glare and make it difficult to see through the glass. the text also mentions that the stress in the glass, left from its heat treatment, can cause the polarization of light to alter. additionally, the text notes that the stress in the rear window changes some of the horizontally polarized light into vertically polarized light, which can pass through the glasses. as a result, the regular pattern of the heat treatment becomes visible.
